name: BLAS OpenMP Thread Control
summary: Align BLAS, OpenMP, and language thread pools with HPC CPU allocations.
description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose CPU oversubscription from implicit math-library and language-runtime thread pools such as OpenMP, oneMKL, OpenBLAS, BLIS, vecLib, NumExpr, R, and Julia, then produce a reviewable export block for Slurm or other HPC batch jobs.

tools.name: python3
tools.notes: Used to report optional NumPy and threadpoolctl runtime details when available.

inputs.name: threads_per_task
inputs.description: Intended CPU threads per process or rank, usually SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK for one threaded task.

inputs.name: outer_parallelism
inputs.description: Description of outer parallelism such as MPI ranks, job array tasks, Dask workers, Ray workers, Python multiprocessing, or data-loader workers.

outputs.name: threadpool_environment_report
outputs.description: Read-only report of scheduler CPU variables, existing thread-related environment variables, optional Python threadpool metadata, and mismatch warnings.

outputs.name: recommended_export_block
outputs.description: Reviewable shell export block for aligning common math-library and language-runtime thread pools with the intended CPU allocation.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and example artifacts.
Generate a read-only local report and export block with two intended threads.
Run in a batch allocation for Python, R, Julia, MATLAB, or compiled BLAS workloads and compare CPU efficiency before and after applying reviewed settings.

references.title: Intel oneMKL techniques to set the number of threads
